author: Iain McGilchrist
text: Consciousness is irreducible, primordial and omnipresent: not a thing, but a creative process.

author: Iain McGilchrist
text: The left hemisphere is a world of atomistic elements; the right hemisphere one of relationships.

author: Iain McGilchrist
text: The model we choose to use to understand something determines what we find.

author: Philip Guston
text: You paint to find out what you're looking at.

author: Philip Guston
text: Painting is an illusion, a piece of magic, so what you see is not what you see.

author: Philip Guston
text: I make a mark, a few strokes, I argue with myself, not do I like or not, but is it true or not?

author: James Lovelock
text: Earth may be alive: not as the ancients saw her—a sentient Goddess with a purpose and foresight—but alive like a tree.

author: James Lovelock
text: We are no more qualified to be the stewards or developers of the Earth than are goats to be gardeners.

author: James Lovelock
text: Keep in mind that it is hubris to think that we know how to save the Earth: our planet looks after itself.

author: David Graeber
text: The ultimate, hidden truth of the world is that it is something that we make, and could just as easily make differently.

author: David Graeber
text: One must oneself, in relations with one's friends and allies, embody the society one wishes to create.

author: David Graeber
text: A human being unable to have a meaningful impact on the world ceases to exist.

author: Gregory Bateson
text: The major problems in the world are the result of the difference between how nature works and the way people think.

author: Gregory Bateson
text: Wisdom is the intelligence of the system as a whole.

author: Gregory Bateson
text: The creature that wins against its environment destroys itself.

author: Ursula K. Le Guin
text: It is good to have an end to journey toward; but it is the journey that matters, in the end.

author: Ursula K. Le Guin
text: You cannot buy the revolution. You cannot make the revolution. You can only be the revolution.

author: Ursula K. Le Guin
text: The creative adult is the child who survived.

author: Brian Wilson
text: Music is God's voice.

author: Brian Wilson
text: I have an instinct for music, or a feeling about it, and I'll have my feelings guide my hands.

author: Brian Wilson
text: We wanted to bring some love to the world.

author: Bill Withers
text: I'm not a star. I'll never be a star. I'm just a messenger. The reason I'm singing is to give hope to people.

author: Bill Withers
text: The only people who can really drain your energy are people you care about.

author: Bill Withers
text: You can't get too high for somebody to bring you down.

author: Ram Dass
text: Be here now.

author: Ram Dass
text: We're all just walking each other home.

author: Ram Dass
text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.

author: Dalai Lama
text: Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.

author: Dalai Lama
text: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.

author: Dalai Lama
text: If you want others to be happy, practice compassion. If you want to be happy, practice compassion.

author: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
text: Music is the bridge between the spiritual and sensual life.

author: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
text: My voice is a gift from Allah, and I must use it to bring people closer to truth and love.

author: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
text: Through music, we can transcend all boundaries and reach the divine.

author: Carl Sagan
text: We are made of star-stuff.

author: Carl Sagan
text: For small creatures such as we the vastness is bearable only through love.

author: Carl Sagan
text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.

author: Rumi
text: What you seek is seeking you.

author: Rumi
text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.

author: Rumi
text: You are not a drop in the ocean, you are the entire ocean in a drop.

author: Thich Nhat Hanh
text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.

author: Thich Nhat Hanh
text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.

author: Thich Nhat Hanh
text: The best way to take care of the future is to take care of the present moment.

author: Terence McKenna
text: Nature loves courage. You make the commitment and nature will respond by removing impossible obstacles.

author: Terence McKenna
text: You are an explorer, and you represent our species, and the greatest good you can do is to bring back a new idea.

author: Terence McKenna
text: Astonishment is the proper response to reality.

author: Octavia Butler
text: All that you touch you change. All that you change changes you.

author: Octavia Butler
text: The only lasting truth is change.

author: Octavia Butler
text: In order to rise from its own ashes, a phoenix first must burn.

author: John Muir
text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than they seek.

author: John Muir
text: The mountains are calling and I must go.

author: John Muir
text: When we try to pick out anything by itself, we find it hitched to everything else in the universe.

author: Adrienne Maree Brown
text: We are in a time of new suns.

author: Adrienne Maree Brown
text: Small is good, small is all.

author: Adrienne Maree Brown
text: What we pay attention to grows.

author: Audre Lorde
text: Your silence will not protect you.

author: Audre Lorde
text: There is no such thing as a single-issue struggle because we do not live single-issue lives.

author: Audre Lorde
text: The master's tools will never dismantle the master's house.

author: Alan Watts
text: You are an aperture through which the universe is looking at and exploring itself.

author: Alan Watts
text: We do not come into this world; we grow out of it.

author: Alan Watts
text: The meaning of life is just to be alive.

author: David Abram
text: We are human only in contact with what is not human.

author: David Abram
text: Humans are tuned for relationship.

author: David Abram
text: Language is less a human possession than it is a property of the animate earth itself.

author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
text: All flourishing is mutual.

author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
text: Attention is a form of reciprocity.

author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
text: Gifts create relationships of responsibility.

author: Niels Bohr
text: The opposite of a profound truth may well be another profound truth.

author: Niels Bohr
text: How wonderful that we have met with a paradox. Now we have some hope of making progress.

author: Niels Bohr
text: Every sentence I utter must be understood not as an affirmation, but as a question.
